name: Swedish Rye Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in 1/4 cup of lukewarm water.
2. Combine 2 cups of water with corn oil, sugar, molasses, and salt in a large bowl.
3. Add the dissolved yeast to the mixture and stir well.
4. Gradually add rye flour and white flour, stirring until a smooth dough forms.
5. Turn the dough out onto a well-floured surface and cover. Let rest for 10 minutes.
6. Knead the dough until it is smooth and satiny, about 10 minutes.
7. Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
8. Punch down the dough, cover, and let rise until almost doubled, about 30 minutes.
9. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and divide into two equal parts.
10. Form each part into balls and cover. Let rest for 15 minutes.
11. Shape the loaves into round shapes, place on greased baking sheets, cover, and let rise until almost doubled in size, about 1 hour.
12. Brush the tops of the loaves with slightly beaten egg.
13. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 40 minutes.
14. Remove from racks to cool.
